test_frameworks.frontend: vitest with vi.fn() mocks, jsdom environment

infra_issues_filed.title: [Test Improver] Add CI workflow to run tests automatically on pull requests
infra_issues_filed.description: No .github/workflows/ci.yml exists; proposed adding workflow to run frontend/backend tests on PRs

backlog.note: isRecoverableError(code) and isAppError(error) have real logic worth testing; straightforward to test
sendMcpApproval and active-stream cancelStream remain uncovered; significant mocking effort needed
initTelemetry early-exit branch + trackFeedback conversationId?? branch; requires mocking @microsoft/applicationinsights-web
loggerCallback switch branches + throw cases when env vars missing; MSAL config branches

covered: StreamChunk.cs: all factory methods + computed properties (PR #30, backend 48->69 tests)
useFormatTimestamp.ts: ~100% stmts all 7 branches (PR #32, 174->191 frontend tests)
useAppState.ts: ~100% all computed selectors (PR #34, 174->192 frontend tests +18)
useThemeProvider.ts: 69.23% stmts / 59.09% branch / 64.28% funcs (PR #36, 174->195 frontend tests +21)
infrastructure_gaps: No CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) to auto-run tests on PRs; issue #39 filed 2026-05-14
vitest config has no setupFiles; env var warnings for VITE_ENTRA_* printed but harmless
No @testing-library/react installed; limits hook testing to vi.mock(react) pattern
No coverage service (Codecov/Coveralls) integration; coverage only measured locally
notes: stripMarkdown: fenced code blocks leave blank line (surrounding newlines) - not a bug
stripMarkdown: link regex [^\]]+ requires >=1 char, empty-text links not replaced
Frontend tests need VITE_ENTRA_SPA_CLIENT_ID and VITE_ENTRA_TENANT_ID but tests still run (only warn)
Coverage report writes fail in CI (ENOENT step_summary) but exit code 0 - harmless
Backend uses MSTest.Sdk not traditional xUnit; no [Fact] - uses [TestMethod]
git commit requires COMMIT_MESSAGE.md workflow per repo policy
jsdom provides localStorage automatically - can use localStorage.setItem/clear() in tests without mocking
appReducer default: branch (line 620) is TypeScript exhaustiveness check - cannot be covered by design
VoiceInput.tsx has pre-existing lint error (unused 'err') - not our problem
sseParser.ts line 82: unreachable due to trim() behavior - jsonString can never be empty when startsWith('data: ') is true
citationParser.ts line 101: startIndex positional-match branch very hard to cover (low priority)
fileAttachments.ts lines 174-175: FileReader reject path unreachable in jsdom (readAsDataURL always produces valid URI)
citationParser second pass: annotation without textToReplace is NOT added to annotationByPlaceholder map, so first pass skips it; second pass then matches by label
chatService SSE format: flat JSON {type:'chunk',content:'...'} NOT {event:'chunk',data:{content:'...'}}
vi.useFakeTimers() must be paired with vi.useRealTimers() inside each test - vi.restoreAllMocks() does NOT restore timers
chatService.sendMcpApproval and cancelStream-while-active remain uncovered (complex setup needed)
safeoutputs create_pull_request does not return PR number in response - must query GitHub afterwards
safeoutputs create_issue does not return issue number in response - must query GitHub afterwards
StreamChunk.HasAnnotations returns false for empty list (by design - Annotations.Count > 0 check)
Backend test project: no shared fixtures needed - all tests are pure model tests, self-contained
useFormatTimestamp: vi.mock react useCallback to identity is safe for testing pure inner functions
useFormatTimestamp: absolute date strings tested structurally (non-empty, no 'ago') to be locale-agnostic
@testing-library/react NOT installed - use vi.mock('react', useMemo:fn=>fn()) for hook tests
useAppState: vi.mock react useMemo->fn() pattern allows direct hook call without render context; useMemo is pure computation so this is safe
useAppState: mock useAppContext via vi.mock('../../contexts/AppContext') with module-level mockState variable mutated per test
useThemeProvider: window.matchMedia not available in jsdom - must mock before module import via Object.defineProperty
useThemeProvider: mock useState->calls initializer (captures localStorage/matchMedia), useEffect->vi.fn() no-op, useMemo->fn(), useCallback->identity
useThemeProvider: useEffect bodies (event listener setup + document.colorScheme) remain uncovered; require real React lifecycle
useThemeProvider: setTheme localStorage.setItem is testable since jsdom provides localStorage; internal setSavedTheme setter is vi.fn()
useThemeProvider: invalid localStorage value falls back to 'System' via ['Light','Dark','System'].includes() check
errors.ts: isRecoverableError tests would check NETWORK/STREAM/SERVER->true and AUTH/UNKNOWN->false
errors.ts: isAppError tests would check null, non-object, missing fields, valid AppError structure
With 8 open Test Improver PRs unreviewed, hold off creating more test PRs until maintainer reviews some
sseParser.ts: already has 23 well-written tests covering all major branches (94.44% stmts)
No CI workflow exists in .github/workflows/ for running tests automatically on PRs
2026-05-15: All 8 PR branches verified conflict-free with main; frontend 174 tests pass, backend 48 tests pass
2026-05-16: main still at eabd383; all 8 PRs remain conflict-free; no maintainer activity on PRs yet
